MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-31-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER GREGORY PRUITT'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE OR OTHER APPROPRIATE RELIEF, THE COURT FINDS THAT THE TRIAL COURT SHOULD NOT LOOK AT DISCOVERY DISPUTES IN ADMINISTRATIVE HEARINGS. THE APPELLATE COURT SHOULD DECIDE THEM. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER MELANIE GHENO, CSR NO. 7489; DEPT. 302. (302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion Of Petition For Writ Of Mandate Or Other Appropriate Relief
Off Calendar Aug-31-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Scott N. Kivel, of Law Offices of Scott N. Kivel, Tel. 858.459.3900, for petitioners;
Hadara R. Stanton, of Office of the Attorney General, Tel. 415.703.5561, for respondents.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The parties are required to appear. With regard to PETITIONER GREGORY PRUITT's Petition For Writ Of Mandate Or Other Appropriate Relief, the Court finds that the trial court should not look at discovery disputes in administrative hearings. The Appellate Court should decide them. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/RBU)
